summary:
Claim: Absence of hopping on one leg in a 3-year-old signifies delayed development.  
Evidence: - Hopping on one leg is typically mastered by age 3 and reflects neuromuscular integration. - Other milestones (drawing a square, feeding by spoon, passing a ball) either occur earlier/later or assess different domains (fine motor/cognition/social).  
Answer: A
